def: Opp. of ūṅghe , adv. Here. See idhar .
adv. Here, hither.
s.m. Intimation, suggestion, hint, sign, gesture, motion; search, inquiry, research.

ety: corr. from 'English'; see inglisyā
def: s.f. Pension.
more: n. f. Pension; superannuation pension. iṅglis'yā , n. m. Probably from the English, invalid. An invalid soldier or sipāhī , who receives a pension, or an allotment of land as pension; a pensioner.

def: s.m. = inglisiyā, q.v. s.m. An invalided or pensioned soldier or sipāhī; pensioner. (The word most probably meaut, originally, 'One who receives an allowance from the English.')
s.m. An invalided or pensioned soldier or sipāhī; pensioner. (The word most probably meaut, originally, 'One who receives an allowance from the English.')

ety: i.e. 'English'+stān
def: s.m. England.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِنْگِلِسْتان (ingilistân)

def: s.m. Red lead, minium (syn. sendūr).
more: n. m. Red lead; minium ( saṅdūr ).

def: ( = in), Oblique plur. of the pron. يهہ yěh, 'this.'
pron. These; them.

ety: inf. n. vii of هدم 'to demolish'
def: s.m. Being demolished, being thrown down; demolition, destruction; extermination.
more: (inf. vii. of هدم) s. m. The being demolished, demolition, abolition.

ety: inf. n. vii of حرف 'to turn' (from)
def: s.m. Turning (from), shunning, avoiding; — deflection, declension; declination (of a heavenly body); turning (against), swerving (from allegiance), defection, revolt, rebellion; apostasy, recusancy; recantation; infraction, violation; defect; disease, distemper
more: (inf. vii. of حرف) s. m. 1. Deflection, inversion, change, (met.) proving a turn-coat. 2. Recantation, deviation, apostasy. 3. Declination (in astronomy). 4. A disease, defect, distemper. انحراف کرنا inḥirāf karnā, To turn aside (from), to shun, avoid, abjure, apostatize, recant.
subs: (-se), To turn aside (from), to shun, avoid; to abjure; to decline; to deviate (from); to turn (against), revolt (from), rebel (against); to apostatize.

ety: inf. n. vii of حصر 'to surround'
def: s.m. Being surrounded, being besieged; being straitened; siege, blockade; restraint, restriction.
more: (inf. vii. of حصر) s. m. Surrounding, encircling; restriction, limitation.

def: A dunghill cock ( Harris ).

ety: the Gr. ἐυαγγελιον Arabicized
def: s.m. The Evangel (as opp. to tauret, 'the Pentateuch'); the New Testament, the Gospel.
more: n. f. The New Testament; the Gospel.
s. m. The new testament, the gospel.

more: (inf. iv. of نکر) s. m. Denial, refusal, negation, disallowance. انکار کرنا inkār karnā, To deny, refuse, disallow.
subs: (-se), To deny, disallow, deny all knowledge (of), to disclaim, gainsay, contradict; to refuse, refuse to admit; to object (to); to reject
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِنْکَار (inkār), borrowed from Arabic إِنْكَار (ʔinkār), from أَنْكَرَ (ʔankara).

ety: inf. n. vii of خفّ 'to be light'
def: s.m. Lightness, levity.

ety: inf. n. vii of قبض 'to seize, hold,' &c.

ety: inf. n. vii of قلب 'to turn, change'

more: (inf. vii. of قلب) s. m. Revolution, vicissitude, alteration, change, inversion, transposition, subversion.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِنْقِلَاب (inqilāb), from Arabic اِنْقِلَاب (inqilāb).

ety: inf. n. vii of کسف 'to cause an eclipse'
def: s.m. Being eclipsed (the sun); solar eclipse.

ety: inf. n. vii of قسم 'to divide'
def: s.m. The being divided; division, partition, distribution.
more: (inf. vii. of قسم) s. m. The being divided; division, partition, distribution.

ety: inf. n. vii of کسر 'to break'

more: (inf. vii. of کسر) s. m. The being broken; contrition, humility.

ety: inf. n. vii of کسر 'to break'
def: s.m. The being broken; humility; abjectness; spiritlessness; despondency.

ety: inf. n. vii of کشف 'to manifest,' &c.
def: s.m. Being disclosed; disclosure, exposure, detection.

ety: inf. n. vii of قطع 'to cut off,' &c.
def: s.m. The being cut, sundered, severed, broken off, &c.: separation, disjunction; amputation; extinction, failure, cessation, discontinuance; adjustment.
more: (inf. vii. of قطع) s. m. The being cut or broken off; amputation, extinction, disjunction, separation, failure, adjustment.

ety: inf. n. vii of قود 'to lead,' &c.
def: s.m. Obedience, subjection, submission, compliance, docility, fidelity.
more: (inf. vii. of قود) s. m. Obedience, submission, compliance, fidelity.

ety: inf. n. vii of قضی 'to determine, decree'
def: s.m. The being ended; expiration (of a term), expiry, lapse (of a period of time); — part. adj. Ended, expired, lapsed, elapsed; finished, accomplished, fulfilled.
more: (inf. vii. of قضي) 1. s. m. The being ended; the expiration of any term. 2. adj. Elapsed, ended, fulfilled, accomplished, finished.

def: Truly we (are) returning to Him.

r: innā, in, nā, innā ilaihi rājěʻūn
def: (in+nā), Indeed we, truly we. (It occurs only in Arabic phrases, e.g. innā ilaihi rājěʻūn, 'Truly we return to Him.')
adv. Only; just, at least; indeed, surely.

ety: v.n. fr. عنّ 'to turn away from women'
def: adj. & s.m. Not desirous of women; impotent; weak, frail; — an impotent man; a coward.
more: n. m. 1. One born with a small penis . 2. An impotent person. 3. A coward.
s. (dakh.) Reins of a bridle: see عنان iﻌnān.

def: pron. (dakh.) These, this.
s.m. Men. mankind (opp. of jinn).
s. m. Men and spirits.

ety: inf. n. iv of نصف 'to reach to the middle' (of), &c.
def: s.m. Justice, equity; impartiality, fairness; decision, equitable adjustment
more: n. m. A. نصف taking half. Justice; equity; equitable adjustment; fairness; fair play; impartiality ( niyāo ). Phr. Tbe scales of justice; even handed justice; a fair field and no favor; lex talionis; fiat justitia ruat cœlum. insāf chāh'nā , v. n. To sue for or demand justice; seek redress; claim. insāf se, ba-insāf , adv. With justice; equitably; fairly; justly; rightly; deservedly. insāf kar'nā , v. n. 1. To administer, dispense, or do justice; to do right; to see justice done ( niyāo karnā ). Phr. To hold the scales even; to see fair play; to see one righted; to give every one his due; audi alteram partem . 2. To decide (a case) equitably ( faislah karnā). ba-nazar-i-insāf , For the purpose of justice.
(inf. viii. of وصف) s. m. Description, qualification, praise, excellence.
(inf. iv. of نصف) s. m. Decision (of a cause or question); equity, justice. انصاف طلب inṣāf-t̤alab, Seeking justice.
subs: (-se), To demand or seek justice (from), to seek redress
adv. With justice, justly, equitably, impartially, fairly, rightly, according to desert or merit
adj. & s.m. Seeking justice; — one who seeks justice
To do justice, to do right; to see justice done (to), to dispense justice; to decide or settle a suit in law ( = faiṣla karnā).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِنْصَاف (ʔinṣāf), from أَنْصَفَ (ʔanṣafa).

def: s.m. Man, mankind, human b ing, mortal ( = ādmī)
more: s. m. Man, a human being, mankind.
subs: prov. He is but a human being, he is but a fallible man.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِنْسَان (insān), borrowed from Arabic إِنْسَان (ʔinsān), from Proto-Semitic *ʔināš- (“man, person”).

def: adj. Pertaining or relating to man, human.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian انسانی (insānī), from Arabic إِنْسَانِيّ (ʔinsāniyy).

ety: for A. انسانية
def: s.f. Human nature, humanity; human kindness, affability, politeness, urbanity. (See ādmīyat.)
more: s. f. Humanity, human kindness, affability, politeness, civility.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِنْسَانِیَّت (insāniyyat), from Arabic إِنْسَانِيَّة (ʔinsāniyya).

ety: inf. n. iv of نشأ 'to grow, spring up,' &c
def: s.f. Writing, composition; the belle-lettres; elegance of style; style, diction
more: (inf. iv. of نشأ) s. f. Producing, creating; (common acceptation) writing, composition, elegance of style, the belles lettres. انشا پردازي inshā-pardāz-ī, s. f. Writing, letter writing, composition. انشاطراز inshā-t̤arāz, An elegant writer. انشاطرازي inshā-t̤arāz-ī, s. f. Elegance of composition.
subs: inshā-garī, s.f. Writing, letter writing; composition; elegance of composition.

def: adv. A. ان شاء if God please. If God the most high willeth; Deo volente ; God willing; Please God; by God's grace.

ety: in+shāʼ+allāhu
def: adv. If God the Most High wills, God willing, &c. = inshāʼllāh.
n. f. 1. Writing; letter-writing; composition; authorship. 2. The act of composition; excellence of style.

ety: inf. n. vii of سدّ 'to stop up'
def: s.m. Prevention, hindrance, obstacle, bar, preventive measure.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِنْسِداد, from Arabic اِنْسِدَاد (insidād).

ety: inf. n. vii of صرم 'to cut'
def: s.m. Ending, completion, finishing, accomplishment, conclusion, termination; performance; administration or management (of an estate, &c.).
more: (inf. vii. of صرم Ch. He cut) s. m. The being cut or broken off; finishing, accomplishing, accomplishment, termination, conclusion.

ety: S. इष्टका
def: s.f. Brick-work, building in brick; a structure of brick
subs: lit. 'To strike or sound one brick against another'; to throw down, raze, or demolish a building; to lay in ruins
baj-jānā, To be thrown down, razed, demolished, laid in ruins
To reduce a brick house to dust, to demolish a brick house; — to destroy the prosperity of a house, to reduce to poverty
īṅṭ-kā ghar maṭṭī honā or ho-jānā
To be brought low from a state of prosperity and affluence, to be reduced to poverty
s.m. A boys' game (one boy throws a stone which others try to hit, and he who is successful rides on the back of the first thrower as far as the stone thrown by him).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀇𑀝𑁆𑀝𑀸 (iṭṭā), from Sanskrit इष्टा (iṣṭā), related to इष्टका (íṣṭakā, “brick”).

def: Mag. n. f. S. इष्टका, Pālī, iṭṭhikā , P. خثت A brick. Morī kī īṅṭ chaubāre chaṛhī . Prov. The drain brick is raised to the terrace. (Said of a parvenu). īṅṭ banā'nā, īṅṭeṅ pāth'nā , v. n. To make bricks. īṅṭ se īṅṭ bajā'nā , v. a. lit . To strike one brick against another. To destroy any building; to sap the foundations of; demolish; raze to the ground. Taimūr jis shahar meṅ gayā, īṅṭ se īṅṭ bajā dī. īṅṭ se īṅṭ baj'nā , v. n. To be demolished; to fall to pieces. Īṅṭ se īṅṭ baj gaī . (The city) was razed to the ground. īṅṭ-kārī, īṅṭ kī chināī , n. f. Brick-laying; brickwork. īṅṭ kā ghar maṭṭī kar'nā , v. a. 1. To pull down a brick house. 2. To destroy the fortunes of a house; to reduce to poverty. īṅṭ kā ghar maṭṭī honā , v. n. To be ruined, reduced to poverty. Īṅṭ kā ghar maṭṭī ho gayā . Prov. Saying. īṅṭ mār chaḍḍhākṛā , n. m. Children's game, in which one boy throws a stone to be hit by the others. The one who hits it has a ride on the first thrower's back up to the stone. der īṅṭ kī masjid chunnā , v. n. lit . To make a masjid of one brick and a half. To have one's own church; to have one's own way; to insist on. madarse kī īṅṭ . Fac. An old backward student who sticks in a lower class. Delhi. īṅṭā kho'yā ; E. īṅṭ kor , n. m. Brick dust.

more: (inf. viii. of نہي) 1. s. f. Termination, end, extremity, summit. 2. adj. Finished, completed, prohibited.

v.t. To transmit, transfer, transport, transpose, convey, remove; to dispose of; to depart from, quit, leave; — v.n. To depart this life, to die
s.m. Deed of transfer or conveyance.

An illegal transfer or conveyance.
Transferee; alienee.
s.f. Transfer of a zamīndārī or other property; the property so transferred

A transfer in perpetuity.
An intermediate conveyance.
Assignment of title to another; transfer of a right.
A transfer of property.

A transfer for a term of years.

To transfer; convey; assign; endorse. 3. To sell; vend; dispose of ( bechnā ). 4. To depart; quit; leave ( chhoṛnā ). intiqāl k .,—v. n. To die; expire ( marnā ).
An alienor; one who conveys.
Deed of transfer or conveyance. intiqāl-nāmah-i-zimmah-dārī, yā dain . Deed of liabilities.

ety: inf. n. viii of نقم 'to take revenge'

subs: (-kā), To take revenge (for), to revenge oneself, to retaliate, make reprisals (syn. badlā lenā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِنْتِقَام (intiqām), from Arabic اِنْتِقَام (intiqām), verbal noun of اِنْتَقَمَ (intaqama), from the root ن ق م (n q m).

ety: inf. n. viii of نسب 'to refer (to), &c.
def: s.m. Being related to or descended from; relation; descent, lineage ( = nasab).

ety: inf. n. viii of نشر 'to spread,'
def: s.m. Spreading abroad, divulging, propagation; explanation; scattering, dispersion; confusion.
more: (inf. viii. of نشر) s. m. Spreading abroad, divulging, scattering, dispersion, confusion, propagation, explanation.

ety: inf. n. viii of نظم 'to join, range, string,' &c.

more: (inf. viii. of نظم) s. m. Arrangement, disposition, order, regularity, management, administration, regulation.
subs: (-ko), To give or impart order, method or arrangement (to), to arrange, order, regulate
v.t. To arrange, set in order, adjust, regulate; to make arrangements (for), provide (against); to manage, direct; to plan, devise.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِنْتِظَام (intizām), from Arabic اِنْتِظَام (intiẓām, “order, regularity”). First attested in c. 1678 as Middle Hindi انتظام (antz̤am /⁠intizām⁠/).

ety: inf. n. نظر 'to behold'
def: adj. Expecting, looking out (for).
more: (inf. viii. of نظر Heb. Beheld, observed) s. m. Expectation, looking out for. انتظار کرنا intiz̤ār karnā, To expect, look out for, await. انتظار کش intiz̤ār-kash, adj. Expecting, looking out for.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِنْتِظَار (intizār), borrowed from Arabic اِنْتِظَار (intiẓār), from اِنْتَظَرَ (intaẓara).

ety: inf. n. نظر 'to behold'
def: adj. Expecting, looking out (for).
more: s. f. Same as انتظار q. v. انتظاري کرنا intiz̤ārī karnā, To expect, to look out for.

ety: inf. n. iv of نزل 'to descend'

ety: inf. n. vii of زوي 'to conceal,' &c.

Gifts and honors; rewards and dignities. inٴaām pānā , v. n. To get a prize or reward. Haq na pāve, inaām? E. Prov. He cannot get even his rights, and yet expects a present. inٴaām-patr , n. m. A deed of grant, a document conveying an assignment of rent-free land. inٴaām-dār , n. m. The holder of a rent-free grant. inٴaām denā , v. a. To give or award a prize to; to distribute prizes; to reward merit. inٴaām kā paisā . Prize allowance.
s.f. Misfortune, calamity, &c. ( = آپت āpat, q.v.). — āpad-grasta ( = P. āfat-girifta), part. adj. (f. ā). Involved in calamity or misfortune, unfortunate.
s.m. Thyme; wild marjoram.
part. Wished for, desired, longed for.

ety: inf. n. iv of ورد 'to approach,' &c.

more: (inf. iv. of ورد) s. f. Bringing, adducing, producing.
subs: To bring (a charge against), impute (blame to).

ety: for A. ارادة, inf. n. iv of راد for رود 'to seek, seek after'
def: s.m. Desire, inclination, will; intention, purpose, resolve, determination; aim, object, end, end in view; plan, design; meaning, purport
more: n. m. رَؤدَ bent, inclined. 1. Desire; will; inclimation ( ichchhā ). 2. Intention; mind; design; purpose; aim; intent. Is kām meṅ tumhārā kyā irādah hai? 3. Resolve; resolution; determination. 4. Object; end; view; proposal ( tajwīz ). 5. Destination; goal; resting, or halting place ( ṭhikānā ). Āgre kā irādah hai . 6. Meaning; scope; purport ( murād ). Sarkār kā yeh irādah hai. irādatan' , Ped. for irāde se , adv. 1. With a view to; with an eye to; for the purpose of; with the view of; with the intent. 2. In order to; to the end that; on account of; in pursuance of; pursuant to. 3. Intentionally; designedly; purposely; on purpose; voluntarily; willingly. 4. Deliberately; studiously; knowingly ( jān būjh ke ). irādah kār'nā , v. n. 1. To desire; intend; purpose; propose to oneself; design. 2. To be at; aim at; drive at; to be after; point at; endeavour; try; aspire to or after. 3. To me…
s. m. (inf. iv. of رود) Desire, inclination, wish, purpose, design, plan, intention, meaning. اراده کرنا irādā karnā, To form a design, to make an attempt, to design, intend or purpose.
subs: To desire, intend, purpose, design; to aim (at, — as a goal or destination), to make (for); to be on the point (of); to form a design (against), make an attempt (on); to contemplate, meditate; to propose, devise, plan

ety: acc. of irādat
def: adv. = irāde-se, q.v.s.r. irāda.

prop. n. Name of a territory between Persia and Arabia (it is divided into two portions, viz. ʻirāq-ě-ʻarab, 'Arabian ʻIrāq,' the ancient Babylonia or Chaldea; and ʻirāq-ě-ʻajam, 'Persian ʻIrāq,' comprehending Media).
more: n. prop. A territory which is divided into two portions; عراق عرب iﻌrāḳ-i-aﻌrab, The ancient Babylonia or Chaldea; and عراق عجم iﻌrāḳ-i-aﻌjam, comprehending Media. عراقان or عراقين The two cities of Basra and Kūfa.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عِراق ('irāq), from Arabic الْعِرَاق (al-ʕirāq).

def: = A عراقان ʻirāqān (nom. and obl. dual of ʻirāq), The cities of Basra and Kūfa.
= A عراقين ʻirāqain (nom. and obl. dual of ʻirāq), The cities of Basra and Kūfa.

ety: for A. اراقة, inf. n. iv of راق for روق 'to become clear,' &c.
def: s.f. Pouring out; spilling, shedding; passing urine; urine.

ety: rel. n. fr. ʻirāq
def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to the country called ʻIrāq; belonging to Irāq; Arabian (horse); Parthian; — an Arab horse; — a Parthian.
more: n. m. An Arab horse. Irāqī phūs ṭhaṭhere khaṛe chabāte haiṅ, Gadhe pulāo ke taīṅ lāt mār jāte haiṅ . Nazīr. The Arab horse upon his legs on straw feeds all the day, The sated Ass his rich pulāo with kicks just flings away.
1. adj. Produced in or belonging to iﻌrāḳ (especially applied to a breed of horses from that country). 2. s. m. A Parthian.
wety: From عراق (irāq) + ـی (-ī)

def: s.m. A fabulous garden in Arabia Felix, paradise.
more: s. m. A fabulous garden in Arabia; paradise.

ety: Pehl. erān; Zend airyana, fr. airya = S. arya, ārya
def: prop. n. Persia
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِیرَان (īrān).

def: adj. & s.m. Of or relating to Persia; — a Persian; a man of the Shīʻa sect.
wety: From ایران (īrān, “Iran”) + ـی (-ī)

def: postpos. (gov. -ke), Around, all round, round about, on every side (of), on all sides. (See gird, and cf. ās-pās).
more: adv. Around; all round; on every side; on all sides; round about; about; right and left.

more: (inf. iv. of رفه Heb. Was loose or remiss; lived at ease) s. m. 1. Bestowing affluence, comforting, cherishing. 2. Enjoyment of peace and affluence.

ety: inf. n. of عرف 'to know,' &c.
def: s.m. Knowledge, sciences, wisdom, discernment.
s.f. = ارسی irsī, q.v. s.f. Axle-tree, axle.

ety: inf. n. iv of رجع 'to return'
def: s.m. Causing to return, returning, bringing back; changing; substituting; instituting (a suit).

def: s.m. Act of writing: (N.B. Arabic lexicons recognize no 4th conj. of ارقام)
more: (inf. iv. of رقم Heb. He marked or punctured) s. m. Act of writing. ارقام کرنا irḳām karnā, To write. ارقام هونا irḳām honā, To be written.

s.f. Envy, jealousy; emulation, rivalry; impatience of another's success; spite, ill-will

subs: or karnā (-kī), To be envious or jealous (of); to envy; to rival

s. m. Inheritance, heritage.

more: (inf. iv. of رسل) s. m. Act of sending (especially, a writing, letter, &c.), mission, remittance.
subs: v.t. To send, despatch, forward, remit, transmit
s.m. Invoice of goods, rents, &c. forwarded or despatched.

def: s.m. Act of marching or travelling; departure; death.
more: (inf. viii. of رحل) s. m. Act of marching or travelling; departure, death.

def: s.m. Taking or receiving a thing as a pledge; pledge; hostage. (See رهن rahn.)

def: s.m. (f. īśā), Ruler, lord, master, Supreme Spirit, God; name of Śiva as regent of the north-east quarter (see īśān).
prop. n. Jesus
more: n. m. S. īsh supreme. Jesus. Īsā ba-dīn-i-khud, Mūsā ba dīn-i-khud . Prov. Christ is Christ in his own creed, Moses in his. (They are nothing to men of other creeds).
(Heb. from To save) n. prop. Jesus.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عِیسَیٰ (īsā), borrowed from Arabic عِيسَى (ʕīsā).

ety: rel. n. fr. عيسي ʻīsā
def: adj. & s.m. Christian; — a Christian
more: n. A Christian. īsāī , adj. Christian. īsāī honā , v. n. To become a Christian. mazhab-i-īsāī . The Christian faith or religion.
wety: From عِیسیٰ ('īsā, “Jesus”) + ـی (-ī).

def: adj. & s.m. = ايشان īśān, q.v.
more: n. S. ईशान कोण The north-east quarter.
A name of Siva. ايسان کون īsān kon, s. m. Northeast.

def: s.m. Presenting, offering (a gift); an offering.
s.m. = īśwar.

s.f. = īśwaratā, q.v. s.f. Supremacy, lordship; divineness of nature, divinity, Godhead; divine or Almighty power; the divine faculties of omnipresence, omnipotence, invisibility, &c.
s.m. A poursuivant.

def: = اسپغول ispag̠ol, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اسبغول (isbaġōl).

def: = اسپند ispand, q.v. s.m. Herb of grace; seed of wild rue; seed of the meṅhdī or Lawsonia inermis; a species of mustard seed (the seed is burnt at marriages and births to drive away evil spirits, or to avert the mischievous effects of evil eyes: — also written sipand); syn. kālā dānā).
more: n. m. Peganum harmala . Wat. A species of mustard seed burnt at marriages and births to drive away evil spirits.

def: s.m. Producing, issuing, causing to emanate; appearing.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِصْفَهَان (isfahān), ultimately from Old Persian *spādānaʰ (“army, adj.”) or *spādānām (“of the armies”), from 𐎿𐎱𐎠𐎭 (spādaʰ, “army”).

def: (or ispahān, i.e. sipāh+ān), prop. n. Ispahan, the capital of Persian ʻIrāq (the ancient Parthia).

s.m. Spinach.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْفَنْج (isfanj), borrowed from Arabic إِسْفَنْج (ʔisfanj), borrowed from Ancient Greek σπόγγος (spóngos).

ety: v.n. fr. عشو; see ʻashā

ety: inf. n. iv of سہل 'to become smooth or soft'
def: s.m. Loosening, moving or producing action of (the bowels), purging; an aperient.
more: (inf. iv. of سہل) s. m. Loosening, opening, purging, flux, diarrhœa.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِسْهَال (ʔishāl).

def: n. m. Ped. for dast . Loosening of the bowels; purging.
s.f. Desire, wish, eagerness, inclination, choice.

ety: for A. اشارة, inf. n. iv of شور 'to show or display' (a thing)
def: v.t. To make a sign (with the hand, or head, &c., generally with the words sir-se, &c. expressed); to beckon; to nod; to wink; to nudge; to cast side glances; to point (to), to indicate; to allude (to), refer (to); to signify (a desire or wish for anything to be done), to direct by sign, &c.; to hint, give the cue (to).
more: n. m. شارَ he exhibited. 1. Sign; pointing; touch; nudge; nod; beck; wink; gesture; shrug; gesticulation. Hāth ke ishāre se bulā lo . 2. Cue; hint; inkling; clue. Aqal-mand ko ek ishārah kāfī hai . Prov. A hint is enough for the wise. 3. Dumb-shew; by-play. 4. Love glances; ogling; sheep's eyes ( slang ). Kyoṅke in āṅkhoṅ se gairoṅ ke ishāre dekhūṅ . Momin. How could I bear to see her cast love-glances at another? 5. Mark; trace; direction; indication. 6. Allusion; implication; reference. 7. Inuendo; hint; insinuation. Ishāre kī guftgū jo jāne us se kaho. ishārā kar'nā , v. a. 1. To make a sign; to beckon. Phr. To wave the flag. 2. To wink; nod; beck; nudge. 3. To cast amorous, or side glances. 4. To hint; give the cue or sign; to tip the wink ( slang ). 5. To refer; allude to. 6. To direct; induce; command. Ek bār woh saudā beche, do bar nain laṛāve, Saudā beche, kare ishārah, kitne ko …
s. m. (inf. iv. of شور) Sign, wink, signal, hint, allusion. اسم اشاره ism-i-ishāra, A demonstrative pronoun. اشاره کرنا ishāra karnā, To beckon, make sign or signal, to hint.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِشَارَه (išāra), borrowed from Arabic إِشَارَة (ʔišāra), from أَشَارَ (ʔašāra). Sense 8 is a semantic loan from Punjabi اِشَارَہ (īśārah, “sign; indicator”).

def: s. f. (inf. iv. of شور) Sign, wink, signal, hint, allusion. اسم اشاره ism-i-ishāra, A demonstrative pronoun. اشاره کرنا ishāra karnā, To beckon, make sign or signal, to hint.

ety: for A. اشارة, inf. n. iv of شور 'to show or display' (a thing)
def: v.t. To make a sign (with the hand, or head, &c., generally with the words sir-se, &c. expressed); to beckon; to nod; to wink; to nudge; to cast side glances; to point (to), to indicate; to allude (to), refer (to); to signify (a desire or wish for anything to be done), to direct by sign, &c.; to hint, give the cue (to).
adv. Little, a little, slightly, somewhat.

ety: inf. n. iv of شفق
def: s.m. Tenderness, compassion ( = shafaqat).
wety: From Classical Persian اشفاق (ašfāq, išfāq), from Arabic إِشْفَاق (ʔišfāq), verbal noun of أَشْفَقَ (ʔašfaqa), and أَشْفَاق (ʔašfāq), the plural of شَفَق (šafaq).

ety: inf. n. iv of شہد 'to give evidence'
def: s.m. Taking to witness; adducing as evidence, bringing proof.
more: (inf. iv. of شہادت) s. m. Taking to witness, bringing proof.

ety: inf. n. of عشق 'to love passionately'
def: s.m. Love, excessive love, passion (for, -kā)
more: n. m. 1. Love. 1. Lagā thā ze bas ishq kā usko tīr, Lagī kheṅchne āh Badr-i-munīr . Mīr Hasan. 2. Ishq meṅ shāh o gudā barabar . Prov. In Love beggars and kings are one. 3. Ishq burā jinjāl, kisi se dil na lagānā! Ek to apnā māl khilānā, dīje apnā joban gaṅvānā, tīje honā bad-nām . 2. Mania; passion. Use jue kā ishq hai. ishq allah . A salutation among Mah. faqīrs. Sāīṅ, ishq allah! Ans. Madad Allāh.! Mendicants. ishq-bāz , n. m. A gallant; rake. ishq-bāzī , n. f. Gallantry; amours. ishq-pechā ; Rus. echakpechā , n. m. The American jasmine; Quamoclit vulgaris , Wat. ishq-i-haqīqī , n. m. True love; love of God. ishq-i-majāzī . False or earthly love. ishq hai , ( Slang .) intj. Excellent! well done!

s. m. Love. عشقباز iﻌshḳ-bāz, A gallant, amorous. عشقبازي iﻌshḳ-bāz-ī, s. f. Gallantry, amour, amorousness. عشق پيچا iﻌshḳ-pechā, s. m. The name of a flower (Ipomœa quamoclit) American jasmine. عشق پيشه iﻌshḳ-pesha, Love-practiser, lover. عشق هي iﻌshḳ hai, an exclamation of praise; excellent! well done! praise to! Mīr says:-- عشق انکي عقل کو هي جو ما سواهماري ناچيز جانتي هين نابود جانتي هين iﻌshḳ unkī aﻌḳl ko hai, jo mā siwā hamāre nāchīz jānte haiṅ, nābūd jānte haiṅ, Excellent is their understanding who esteem every thing besides me worthless and null.
subs: 'Love of God,' a form of salutation used (instead of salām) by faqīrs, and by wrestlers when they meet in the arena
adj. & s.m. Amorous; — a gallant; a rake
s.f. Amorous toying, love-making, gallantry, amour
s.m. A species of ivy that bears red flowers; — American jasmine, Ipomæa quamoclit, or Q. vulgaris
adj. & s.m. Amorous; — one practised in love, a lover
An exclamation of praise; excellent! well done! bravo!
ex: تیرے عشق میں ہم نے کیا کیا نہ دیکھا؟
tere iśq mẽ ham ne kyā kyā na dekhā?
What did I not see in your love?
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عِشْق (išq), borrowed from Arabic عِشْق (ʕišq). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi عشق ('śq /⁠'iśq⁠/).

ety: inf. n. iv of شکل 'to become dubious'
def: Ambiguity, difficulty, suspicion, painfulness.
more: (inf. iv. of شکل) s. m. Ambiguity, difficulty, suspicion; painfulness.

def: s.m. One who excites doubts or difficulties.

ety: from ishkāl
def: v.t. To excite suspicion; to deceive, tempt, delude, to wheedle.

ety: rel. n. fr. ʻishq
def: adj. & s.m. Of or relating to love; amatory, erotic; — an amorous man; a lover.
more: adj. Amatory, relating to love; lover.

ety: inf. n. iv of شمّ 'to smell'
def: s.m. Diffusing odour, shedding perfume; (Gram.) giving to a quiescent consonant a slight sound or smack of ẓamma or kasra, but so as not to lengthen the syllable or make an additionable syllable, as in ḵẖẉesh.
more: (inf. iv. of شمّ) s. m. 1. Diffusing odour, shedding perfume. 2. Giving to a quiescent consonant a slight sound of ẓamm or kasr, but so as not to lengthen the syllable or make a syllable more.

ety: inf. n. iv of شرق 'to rise, shine' (the sun, &c.)
def: s.m. Rising (of the sun), sun-rise, morning; splendour, lustre, beauty.
more: n. m. شَرَقَ the sun rose. H. ude , q. v. namāz-i-ishrāq , n. f. Prayer at sunrise; morning prayer.
(inf. iv. of شرق) s. m. Rising (of the sun), splendour, lustre, beauty.

ety: inf. n. iv of شرك 'to share'
def: s.m. Participating; entering into partnership.
more: (inf. iv. of شرکت) s. m. Participating, entering into partnership.

def: adj. Of or pertaining to sunrise, morning; Eastern, oriental; having the splendour of the East

ety: for A. عشرة, fr. muʻāsharat, 'holding social or familiar intercourse,' inf. n. iii of عشر 'to make ten of'
def: s.f. Social or familiar intercourse, pleasant and familiar conversation, society; pleasure, enjoyment, mirth
more: n. f. Pleasure; enjoyment. ashrat-/?/khānā, gāh, yā kadah , v. n. A pleasure chamber; a house of pleasure or entertainment. ashrat manānā , v. n. To enjoy oneself; to revel. Bāheṅ gale meṅ ḍāle ashrat manā rahe haiṅ . Nazīr. His arms thrown round her neck he revels in enjoyment keen.
(vulg. aﻌshrat ) s. f. 1. Society, pleasant and familiar conversation. 2. Pleasure, delight, enjoyment, mirth. عشرت فزا iﻌshrat-fazā, Increasing pleasure.
subs: s.m., or ʻishrat-kada, s.m., or ʻishrat-gāh, s.f. A house of pleasure, a place of entertainment
adj. & s.m. Pleasure-increasing; — one who increases enjoyment or mirth
v.n. To enjoy oneself, to take enjoyment or pleasure, to revel.

def: n. S. इष् to desire, यज् to worship. 1. Faith; trust; belief. Use Devī kā ishṭ hai . 2. One chosen or favoured of a god or goddess.

part. adj. Desired, wished for; approved; cherished, beloved; reverenced, worshipped; — s.m. An object of desire, love or reverence; a god, deity; a beloved person; one chosen or favoured of a god or goddess; faith, trust, love
s.m. & adj. Stamp. (See اسٿام isṭām.)
s.f. Desirableness; belovedness; respect, reverence.

ety: superl. of ishṭa
def: adj. Most desired, best beloved, dearest.

ety: compar. of ishṭa
def: adj. More desired, dearer, more loved.

ety: inf. n. viii of شعل 'to burn'
def: s.m. Burning, blazing; inflaming; lighting a fire or candle; — fomenting, instigating a quarrel.
more: (inf. viii. of شعله) s. m. f. Inflaming, burning, lighting a fire or candle; fomenting or instigating a quarrel.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic اِشْتِعَال (ištiʕāl).

ety: i.e. ishtěʻāl+dim. aff. ak
def: s.f. A small flame; a quarrel
more: s. f. A small flame; a quarrel. اشتعالک دینا ishtiʼāﻌlak denā, v. a. To foment (quarrels), to encourage to bad actions, to abet.
subs: v.t. To foment (quarrels); to incite to bad actions; to abet.

ety: inf. n. viii of شہو 'To be pleasant'
def: s.f. Desire, appetite, longing; hunger.
more: (inf. viii. of شہوت) s. f. Appetite, desire, hunger.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اشتها (ištihā), from Arabic اِشْتِهَاء (ištihāʔ).

ety: inf. n. viii of شہر 'to make public'
def: s.m. Publication, divulging; publicity, public notice; notification, announcement, advertisement, proclamation, declaration, notice, placard, poster; fame, rumour, report, renown
more: (inf. viii. of شہرت) s. m. Publication, divulging, fame, rumour, report, renown. اشتہارکرنا ishtihār karnā, To publish, divulge. اشتہار نامه ishtihār-nāma, An advertisement.
subs: (-ko), To give publicity (to), to notify, announce; to promulgate; to give notice; to publish, proclaim
v.t. To publish, proclaim; to divulge
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِشْتِهَار (ištihār).

def: s.m. Lit. 'One who is advertised'; a person who has absconded.
s.f. Sacrificing, sacrifice; sacrificial pile.

ety: inf. n. viii of شبه 'to assimilate,' &c.
def: s.m. Doubt ( = shubha), suspicion; ambiguity; scruple; — likening; comparison.
more: (inf. viii. of شبه) s. m. Doubt, ambiguity, scruple.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اشتباه (ištibāh), from Arabic verbal noun اِشْتِباه (ištibāh), from شَبَّهَ (šabbaha, “to assimilate”).

def: adj. Doubtful, suspicious; ambiguous.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِشْتِبَاهِی (ištibāhī, “faulty, erroneous”).

ety: inf. n. viii of شد 'to make hard, firm,' &c.
def: s.m. Confirming, strengthening, increasing in violence.
more: (inf. viii. of شدت) s. m. Confirming, strengthening, increasing in violence.

ety: inf. n. viii of شغل 'to busy, to occupy'
def: s.m. Occupation, employment ( = shug̠l), business, study.
more: (inf. viii. of شغل) s. m. Occupation, employment, study, business.

ety: inf. n. viii of شق 'to cleave, split'
def: s.m. Derivation, etymology.
more: (inf. viii. of شقّ) s. m. Derivation of one word from another, etymology.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِشْتِقَاق (ištiqāq).

ety: inf. n. viii of شمل 'to include'
def: s.m. Comprehension, containing.
more: (inf. viii. of شمل) s. m. Comprehension, containing.

ety: inf. n. viii of شري 'to buy; to sell'
def: s.m. Buying; selling; commerce.
more: (inf. viii. of شريٰ) s. m. Buying, selling, commerce.

ety: inf. n. viii of شرك 'to share'
def: s.m. Partnership, fellowship ( = shirkat and sājhā), participation, communion; — (in lexicology) the being homonymous; homonymy.
more: (inf. viii. of شرکت) s. m. Partnership, fellowship.
ex: اُن دنوں تحریکِ عدمِ اشتراکِ عمل زوروں پر تھی۔
In those days the non-cooperation movement was in full swing.
یَہاں تَک تو قَدِیم و جَدِید واقِعات میں تَشابُہ اَور اِشْتِراک ہَے۔
yahā̃ tak to qadīm o jadīd vāqi'āt mẽ taśābu aur iśtirāk hai.
To the extent that there is a resemblance and similarity in the ancient and modern events.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِشْتِرَاک (ištirāk), borrowed from Arabic اِشْتِرَاك (ištirāk), from اِشْتَرَكَ (ištaraka).

ety: inf. n. viii of شوق 'to excite desire'
def: s.m. Desire, strong inclination, longing, craving, yearning; love, fondness
more: (inf. viii. of شوق) s. m. Wish, desire, strong inclination.
subs: (-kā), To be eagerly desirous (of); to long (for); to yearn (after); &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِشْتِیَاق (ištiyāq), from Arabic اِشْتِيَاق (ištiyāq).

def: n. f. 1. Divine or Almighty power or supremacy. 2. The divine faculties of omnipresence, omnipotence, invisibility, etc.
s.f. = S ايشتو ईशित्व īśitwa, s.m. Superiority, supremacy, lordship; one of the eight attributes of the Deity.
s.m. = S ايشتا ईशिता īśitā, s.f. Superiority, supremacy, lordship; one of the eight attributes of the Deity.
To this degree, thus much, so much, thus.
prop. n. Alexander.
adj. Of or relating to Alexander.
more: adj. Of or relating to Alexander.

def: prop. n. Alexandria (in Egypt).
wety: From Arabic الْإِسْكَنْدَرِيَّة (al-ʔiskandariyya).

def: s. m. A chapter, a division of a book.

ety: S. स्कन्ध
def: s.m. Section (of a book), book, chapter.

ety: inf. n. iv of سقط 'to fall, drop'
def: s.m. Causing to fall; dropping or casting her young (an animal); miscarrying, miscarriage; procuring an abortion
more: n. m. سقط it fell. Causing to fall; casting her young (a camel). isqāt-i-hamal , n. m. Causing to miscarry; procuring an abortion; miscarriage; abortion ( peṭ girānā ).
(inf. iv. of سقط Heb. and Ch. He fell, &c.) s. m. Causing to fall; procuring an abortion, miscarrying, miscarriage, taking away, dying (of cattle).
subs: s.m. Miscarriage; procuring an abortion; abortion.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِسْقَاط (ʔisqāṭ).

ety: inf. n. iv of سکت 'to become still or quiet'
def: s.f. Silencing; pacifying, soothing, quieting, assuaging, allaying.
more: (inf. iv. of سکوت) s. f. Pacifying, soothing, silencing.

ety: Gr. σχίλλα Arabicized
def: s.m. A squill, a wild onion.

ety: inf. n. iv of سلو 'to experience comfort'
def: s.m. Comfort, consolation.
more: (inf. iv. of سلا or سلي) s. Comfort, consolation: see تسلي tasallī.

ety: inf. n. iv of صلح 'to be good,' &c.
def: s.f. Correction, improvement, amendment, emendation, adjustment; revision; — dressing or trimming the beard; the hair on the chin
more: (inf. iv. of ضلح Heb. and Ch. Prospered, was fit) s. f. Correction, amendment, emendation. اصلاح کرنا iṣlāḥ karnā, To correct, to amend.
subs: To trim or dress the beard; to shave the chin
adj. Capable of correction or remedy, remediable
To be remediable; to be or become corrected
v.t. To correct, amend, improve, rectify, reform, cure; to correct or improve penmanship or composition
(-se), To be corrected (by); to have (a thing, especially writing or composition) corrected (by).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِصْلَاح (ʔiṣlāḥ).

ety: inf. n. iv of سلم 'to be or become safe'
def: s.m. The Mohammadan religion; orthodoxy (according to Moḥammadans)
more: n. m. سلم he became secure. The religion of Mohammad ; orthodoxy according to Mahomedans. islām lānā,—qubūl k. , v. n. To be converted to Mahomedanism; to profess the Mahomedan faith.
(inf. iv. of سلم Heb. and Ch. Was safe, entire, at peace) s. m. The Muhammadan religion, orthodoxy (according to Muhammadans), islāmism.
subs: islām qubūl karnā, v.n. To profess the Moḥammadan religion; to be converted to Moḥammadanism.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْلَام (islām), borrowed from Arabic إِسْلَام (ʔislām), from أَسْلَمَ (ʔaslama).

def: adj. Faithful; orthodox (according to Moḥammadans); — s.m. A follower of the Moḥammadan religion, a Muslim.
more: adj. Faithful; orthodox (according to the Mahomedans). islāmī , n. m. A follower of the Mahomedan religion.
s. m. f. A Muhammadan, a believer. (Pers. plur. اسلامیان islāmiyān ).
wety: From اسلام (islām, “Islam”) + ـی (-ī)

def: adj. Faithful, orthodox.
For this reason, hence, because.
conj. v.s.v. اس is.

ety: rt. سمو
def: s.m. Name, appellation; (in Gram.) a noun
more: n. m. H. nām . 1. Name; denomination; appellation. 2. In Gram . A noun or substantive. ism-i-istifhām , H. puchhvaīyā nām palaṭ , An interrogative pronoun. ism-i-ishārah , S. संकेत H. batāū nām palaṭ , A demonstrative pronoun. ism-i-āzam , The name of the Almighty; one of the ninety-nine attributes of the Divinity, the utterance of which is an irresistible spell over demons. Mah. ism-i-tankīr , H. gol nām palāṭ , An indefinite pronoun. ism-jāmid , A primitive noun. ism-jalālī , The glorious name of God. ism-jins , H. jāt nām , A generic name. ism-hālyā , H. chalat kiryā nām , The present participle continuative. ism-sifat , H. bhāo, gun , An adjective. ism-i-zamir , S. सर्वनाम H. nām palaṭ , A pronoun. ism-i-adad , H. gintī bhāo , A numeral adjective. ism-i-fāil , H. kartā. In Gram . The agent or subject. ism-i-farzī , H. dharā nām , Feigned name; assumed name; fictitious name. ism-kul'l…

s. m. Name, a noun. اسم با مسميٰ ism bā musammā, Name coinciding with the named, or name fitly denoting the qualities of the named. اسم جامد ism-i-jāmid, A noun primitive. اسم جلالي ism-i-jalāl-ī, The name of God. اسم جنس ism-i-jins, A noun appellative or generick. اسم حالیه ism-i-ḥāliya, Present participle. اسم صفت ism-i-ṣifat, A concrete noun or adjective. اسم نویسي ism-nawīsī, s. f. A list or roll of names, a catalogue, file, muster-roll. (Plur. اسمأ asmā ).
subs: Lit. 'The great name'; the name of the Almighty; one of the ninety-nine names or attributes of God, the utterance of which is an irresistible spell over evil spirits
A name fitly denoting the qualities of the named
An aplastic noun, a primitive noun
A name importing something good or beautiful
The present participle; the noun indicating the state or condition of the subject
s.f. List, roll or register of names, muster-roll; the act of enrolling; registry-office
s.f. List of witnesses; petition to summon witnesses
adj. & adv. Entry (in statements) according to the order of individual names.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْم (ism), borrowed from Arabic اِسْم (ism).

def: prop. n. Ishmael, son of Abraham.
more: n. prop. Ishmael son of Abraham.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْمَاعِیل (ismā'īl), from Arabic إِسْمَاعِيل (ʔismāʕīl), from Hebrew יִשְׁמָעֵאל (yishma'él, yišmāʿēl).

def: n. prop. (Heb. ) Ishmael.

ety: for A. عصمة, inf. or v.n. of عصم 'to defend,' &c.
def: s.f. Defence, protection, preservation (esp. fr. sin); — honour, integrity; continence, chastity; the parda or seclusion in which (oriental) women are required to live.
more: n. f. عصم preserved. 1. Chastity. 2. The pardah (screen) or seclusion in which women are required to live.
(from عصم Heb. To be strong, to strengthen, to protect or defend, to preserve from sin) s. f. 1. Defence, protection (especially from sin); hence, 2. Chastity, honour, integrity, continence.

def: adj. Nominal (as opposed to verbal); by name, named.
n. m. A primitive or concrete noun, not derived from any root and from which no word is derived.
adj. f. = اسمی ismī, q.v. (e.g. jumlaʼi-ismīya, a nominal sentence). adj. Nominal (as opposed to verbal); by name, named.

ety: S. स्मृति
def: s.f. The Hindū law. (See smr̤iti.)
v.s.v. اسم ism.
Twelve; the twelve Imāms. اٿنا عشري is̤nā-aﻌshar-ī, adj. Relating to the twelve (Imāms).
adj. (f. -a), Twelve; the twelve Imāms.
adj. Relating to the twelve Imāms.

ety: inf. n. iv of سند 'to lean' (upon)
def: s.m. Resting a thing upon (another); alleging on the authority of another.
n. prop. See اسفہان isfahān.

s.m. Herb of grace; seed of wild rue; seed of the meṅhdī or Lawsonia inermis; a species of mustard seed (the seed is burnt at marriages and births to drive away evil spirits, or to avert the mischievous effects of evil eyes: — also written sipand); syn. kālā dānā).
more: s. m. A seed burnt at marriages to drive away evil spirits: it is, also, burnt as a charm for the like purpose some days after a child is born, particularly at the door; and when a child goes out or comes home. It is often mixed on those occasions with mustard seed, and hence has been confounded with the latter (خردل).
s. m. A seed (that of the مينہدي meṅhdī, q. v. Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: On this. ¶ us-par, On that.

ety: S. ईश्वर+मूल
def: s.m. The plant Indian birthwort, Aristolochia Indica. (It is said to be a valuable antidote to snake-bites, being applied both externally and internally

ety: Port. espada
def: s.m. Steel (syn. faulād).
more: (perhaps from the Portuguese espada) s. m. Steel (of a tough kind).
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese espada, from Latin spatha, from Ancient Greek σπάθη (spáthē).

ety: S. स्पृहा, the a being red. in Hindī
def: s.f. Desire, wish, inclination.

ety: inf. n. iv of سرف 'to be negligent,' &c.
def: s.m. Excess, extravagance, profusion, prodigality, lavish expenditure, abuse of wealth; dissipation, ruin.
more: n. f. سرف wasting. Abuse of wealth; extravagance; lavish expenditure; excess of expenditure ( fazūl- kharchī ).
(inf. iv. of سرف) s. m. Prodigality, dissipation, abuse of wealth, consumption, extravagance, ruin.
wety: From Arabic إِسْرَاف (ʔisrāf).

ety: inf. n. iv of صرف 'to turn'; to exchange; to expend'
def: s.m. Expenditure; waste, extravagance, prodigality
more: (inf. iv. of صرف) s. m. Expenditure, waste, prodigality. اصراف کرنا iṣrāf karnā, v. a. To squander, dissipate, &c.
subs: v.t. To expend; to squander, waste, dissipate, &c.

def: s.m. The name of an angel who, it is said, will sound the last trumpet at the resurrection; a seraph.
more: s. m. 1. The name of an angel who, it is said, will sound the last trumpet at the resurrection. 2. A seraph.

def: prop. n. Israel.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِسْرَائِيل (ʔisrāʔīl), itself from Hebrew יִשְׂרָאֵל (yisrá'él).

ety: inf. n. iv of سرّ 'to gladden'
def: s.m. Keeping secret; concealment, suppression; secrecy, secret, mystery; influence of an evil spirit
more: n. m. 1. The act of concealment. 2. Secret; mystery. Khudā jāne is meṅ kyā isrār hai? 3. The influence of an evil spirit Ise ūpar-wālioṅ kā isrār hai .
(inf. iv. of سرّ) s. 1. Concealment, keeping secret. 2. Disclosure, revealing of a secret.
wety: From Arabic أَسْرَار (ʔasrār), plural of سِرّ (sirr).

ety: inf. n. iv of صرّ 'to make a prolonged sound,' &c.
def: s.m. Persevering; persisting (in); perseverance; obstinacy.
more: (inf. iv. of صرّ) s. m. Persisting, perseverance (especially in something bad), obstinacy.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِصْرَار (ʔiṣrār).

def: s.m. Minium, cinnabar.
s.m. = ايشور īśwar, q.v.
more: n. m. S. ईश्वर from ईश to rule, Pr. issaro, īsaro God. 1. The Almighty God; Lord; the Supreme Being. 2. One of the three presiding Hindū Divinities. 3. Prosperity; good fortune; the smiles of fortune. 1. Issar āë, daliddar jāë . E. Prov. Come good fortune, go poverty. 2. Issar se bheṭā nahīṅ, daliddar se bigāṛ . E. Prov. A stranger to fortune, he quarrels with misfortune.

ety: Bar. Gr. σταβλιον; Lat. stabulum
def: s.m. A stable.
s.m. A stable (see اسطبل ist̤abal).
ex: گھوڑے کو اصطبل میں لےکر کھانا دو۔
ghoṛe ko astabal mẽ lekar khānā do.
Take the horse to the stable and give it some food.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اصطبل (istabl), from Arabic إِصْطَبْل (ʔiṣṭabl), via Byzantine Greek στάβλον (stáblon) from Latin stabulum.

ety: P. ستبره sitabra, Arabicised
def: s.m. Thick silk brocade interwoven with gold; silk or satin (particularly green) the colour of which seems to change agreeably to the light in which it is seen.
more: s. m. Silk cloth or satin, the colour of which seems to change agreeably to the light it is seen in (particularly, green silk).

ety: v.n. of istādan, 'to stand' = Pehl. ostātan, from Z. stā = S. स्था
def: part. adj. = استاده istāda, q.v. part. adj. Erected, set up; standing; erect; — s.m. Pole, ensign-staff; stay, support, prop (especially) a prop for supporting the door of a tent, or a canopy.
more: s. f. The act of standing. ¶ part. (for استاده) Standing, raised up.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اُسْتَاد (ustād). Doublet of اُسْتاذ (ustāz).

ety: perf. part. of īstādan; Pehl. ośṭātan, rt. Zend. stā = S. sthā
def: part. adj. Erected, set up; standing
subs: v.t. To raise up, to erect, &c.

ety: perf. part. of istādan, 'to stand'; see istād
def: part. adj. Erected, set up; standing; erect; — s.m. Pole, ensign-staff; stay, support, prop (especially) a prop for supporting the door of a tent, or a canopy.
more: part. Standing, set up, erected, erect; (hence, sub.) A pole, ensign-staff, prop, &c. particularly, a prop for supporting the door of a tent, and not the tent pole.

ety: abst. subst. from next
def: s.f. Erection; stability.

ety: abst. subst. from istāda, q.v.
def: s.f. Standing, stand, stop, stay, erection; firmness, resistance.
more: s. f. Standing, stand, erection, firmness, resistance.

def: n. m. Stamp ( ṭikaṭ ). isṭām-farosh , n. m. Stamp vender. isṭām kā kāgaz , n. m. Stamped paper. isṭām lagā huā, pospid (Post paid), Ped. ṭikaṭ chaspāṅ , adj. Bearing stamp; stamped. isṭām musbata , Ped. adj. Stamped.
s.m. (corr. of the English), Stamp; postage-stamp (syn. ṭikaṭ)
s. m. The trunk of a tree, a column, pile, post, pillar.
(ειστηνπολιν), prop. n. The city of Constantinople (also written استانبول istāmbol).
n. prop. Constantinople.
s.m. (corr. of the English), Stamp; postage-stamp (syn. ṭikaṭ)
s.m. (corr. of the English), Assistant.
n. m. An assistant.
s.m. A weight of four and a half mis̤qāls or six and a half drachms.
more: s. A weight of four and a half mis̤ḳāl. Four (in number).

def: This manner, (in) this manner, thus.
In this manner, thus.

ety: στύραξ Arabicised
def: s.m. Storax.

ety: S. स्तृ 'spreading out'
def: s.f. Smoothing-iron; a tailor's goose
more: n. f. A tailor's goose; a smoothing iron. istrī kar'nā , v. a. To iron; to smooth with the iron.
subs: v.t. To smooth with a heated iron, to iron
s.m. A person who irons linen.
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese estirar.

ety: inf. n. x of عون; iv. اعان 'to help'
def: s.f. Seeking assistance, praying for help (ʻaun); aid, help, assistance ( = ʻaun); protection.
more: (inf. x. of عون) s. f. 1. Asking assistance, imploring help. 2. Assistance, protection.

ety: inf. n. x of عور; iv. اعار 'to lend'
def: s.m. Asking a loan (ʻārīyat), borrowing; using a word metaphorically; metaphor, figure

ety: inf. n. x of عدّ; iv. اعدّ 'to make ready,' &c.
def: s.f. Getting oneself ready; readiness, preparation; proficiency; ability, means; capability, capacity, mental power, talent, parts; aptitude, dexterity.
more: (inf. x. of عدّ) s. f. Readiness, preparation, proficiency, ability, means, capacity.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِسْتِعْدَاد (istiʕdād).

ety: inf. n. x of عفو 'to efface'
def: s.m. Seeking to be excused (from); asking forgiveness, deprecating; resignation (of an office or appointment)
more: (inf. x. of عفو) s. m. Asking forgiveness, deprecating.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْتِعْفَا (isti'fā), borrowed from Arabic اِسْتِعْفَاء (istiʕfāʔ).

ety: inf. n. x of حول 'to be altered or changed'
def: s.m. Change of state, undergoing a change; becoming, or deeming, absurd, inconsistent, unreal or impossible.
more: (inf. x. of حول) s. m. Accounting or being impossible or absurd: changing of state.

ety: inf. n. x of حيض 'to menstruate'
def: s.m. Immoderate flux of the menses; menstrual discharge at an undue period or time.
more: (from حیض) s. m. Immoderate flux of the menses.

ety: inf. n. x of حبّ 'to be loved'
def: s.m. Contracting friendship; — an action the performance of which is meritorious and the omission not criminal; work of supererogation.
more: (inf. x. of حبّ Heb. He loved) s. m. 1. Contracting friendship. 2. An action the performance of which is meritorious and the omission not criminal; work of supererogation.

ety: inf. n. x of حق 'to be or become just, right,' &c.
def: s.m. Demanding as a right or due, demanding justice; claim, demand, right, due, title; merit; ability
more: (inf. x. of حقّ Heb. Was prescribed or necessary) s. m. Demanding justice, claim, right, merit, ability.
subs: Equity of redemption of a mortgage
Right of preëmption, preëmptive title ( = ḥaqq-i-shufʻa)
Right of way; exercise or use of a right

ety: inf. n. x of حکم 'to restrain,' &c.
def: s.m. Firmness, strength; confirmation, corroboration, ratification; fixity
more: (inf. x. of حکم Heb. Was firm, was wise) s. m. Confirmation, corroboration, firmness, strength, fastness.
subs: (-ko), To strengthen, make firm; to confirm; to corroborate; to ratify, sanction, make valid or legal; to verify, attest.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْتِحْکَام (istihkām), from Arabic اِسْتِحْكَام (istiḥkām).

ety: inf. n. x of حقر 'to be contemptible,' &c.
def: s.m. Treating with contempt, despising, scorning, vilifying.
more: (inf. x. of حقارت) s. m. Treating with contempt, vilifying, scorning, despising.

ety: inf. n. x of حلف 'to swear'
def: s.m. Causing to swear; exacting an oath.

ety: inf. n. x of هلك 'to destroy'
def: s.m. Seeking the death or ruin of any one; — ruining, destroying, consuming ( = halāk).
more: (inf. x. of هلاک) s. m. 1. Seeking the death or ruin of any one. 2. Ruining, destroying, consuming.

ety: inf. n. x of هلّ 'to appear' (the new moon)
def: s.m. The appearing of the new moon (hilāl).
more: (inf. x. of هلال) s. m. The appearing of the new moon.

ety: inf. n. x of حسن 'to be good,' &c.
def: s.m. Approving, praising; taking or regarding as a favour.

ety: inf. n. x of هزأ 'to laugh at,' &c.
def: s.m. Laughing at; derision, scorn, scoff; jest, joke.
more: (inf. x. of هزا) s. m. Derision, laughing at, joke, jest, scorn, scoff.
wety: From Arabic اِسْتِهْزَاء (istihzāʔ).

ety: inf. n. x of حضر 'to become present'
def: s.m. Calling, citing to appear, summoning before.
more: (inf. x. of حضور) s. m. Calling, citing to appear, summoning before.

ety: inf. n. x of عجل 'to make haste'
def: s.m. Wishing to make haste; requiring haste or expedition (from a person, or in a matter); hastening, accelerating.
more: (inf. x. of عجل) s. m. Wishing to make haste, ordering one to make haste, hastening, accelerating, despatch.

ety: inf. n. x of علو 'to be high'
def: s.m. Superiority; exaltation; seeking promotion.
more: (inf. x. of علو) s. m. Superiority, exaltation, seeking promotion.

ety: inf. n. x of عمل 'to work, serve'
def: s.m. Work, operation; employment, use; practice, exercise; custom, usage, observance; fashion
more: (inf. x. of عمل Heb. He operated) s. m. Custom, daily practice, exercise, use. استعمال کرنا istiﻌmāl karnā, To use.
subs: v.n. To be in use, be current (a word, or phrase)
(-kā), To make use (of); to use, employ, put in use, apply
v.n. To come into use, to be in use, be used
(-ko), To bring into use, put in use or in operation, put in practice; to apply; to use, employ ( = istěʻmāl karnā); to utilize
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian استعمال (isti'māl), from Arabic اِسْتِعْمَال (istiʕmāl).

def: adj. Used; worn, second-hand; usual, customary; fit for use; practical: — s.m. The best kind of rice.
more: adj. 1. Used; worn; old. Istemālī chīz kī ādhī qīmat . 2. Customary; usual; practical. istemālī , n. m. The best kind of rice.
adj. Used, usual. ¶ s. m. Fine rice.

def: s.m. (corr. of the English), Railway station; police station.
wety: Borrowed from English station.

def: n. m. Railway or Police station.

ety: S. स्थापित
def: s.m. Setting up or placing an idol in a temple.

ety: S. स्थिर
def: adj. Standing, stagnant; stable, steady, fixed, firm, settled, immovable.
adj. S. स्थिर from स्था to place. Stable; stagnant; immovable.
s.f. = ستهرتا स्थिरता, sthiratā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. x of وعب 'to take the whole,' &c.
def: s.m. Taking the whole; extirpating; asking in a present.
more: (inf. x. of وعب) s. m. Taking the whole, extirpating; asking in a present.

def: (inf. x. of عاریت) s. m. Borrowing; using a word metaphorically; a metaphor. استعاره کرنا istiāﻌra karnā, To beseech, to beg earnestly.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic اِسْتِعَارَة (istiʕāra).

ety: inf. n. viii of صبغ 'to dye; to dip
def: s.m. Dyeing, staining; dipping, immersion, baptism ( = baptizma).
more: (inf. viii. of صبغ Heb. and Ch. He stained) s. m. Imbuing, staining; dipping, baptism.

ety: inf. n. x of بدّ 'to remove or withdraw with'
def: s.m. Absolute dominion, despotism; insisting upon a thing being done.
more: (inf. x. of بدّ) s. m. Absolute dominion, despotism, insisting upon a thingʼs being done.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْتِبْدَاد (istibdād), borrowed from Arabic اِسْتِبْدَاد (istibdād), from اِسْتَبَدَّ (istabadda).

ety: inf. n. x of برأ 'to become clear or free of'
def: s.m. Cleansing; washing one's hands (of a business); desiring to be freed (from a business); the waiting of a woman for a certain time (three months according to Mohammadan law) after being divorced from her husband, to ascertain whether she is pregnant or not, before she can contract another marriage.
more: (inf. x. of برأ) s. m. 1. Cleansing, washing oneʼs hands of a business or desiring to be freed from it. 2. The waiting of a woman after being divorced from her husband, to as certain whether she is pregnant or not, before she can contract another marriage. (The term is three months, according to the Muhammadan law).

ety: inf. n. x of دعو 'to seek, pray for,' &c.
def: s.f. Asking, supplicating; desire; request, demand; claim; supplication, prayer, humble petition.
more: (inf. x. of دعوت) s. f. Request, desire, invitation, supplication, application.
